metadata.website_type: local mental health counseling services website

tasks.name: Book the earliest in-person anxiety counseling session after 5 pm within the next 7 days
tasks.description: Use the scheduling tool to find an in-person individual counseling appointment for anxiety near ZIP 94103, and book the earliest available time slot at or after 5:00 pm on any date within the next 7 calendar days.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Book an Appointment" link in the main navigation menu.
On the appointment type selection page, click the "Individual Counseling" option.
In the concerns or specialties filter panel, select the checkbox labeled "Anxiety".
In the format/location filters, select the "In-person" option and type "94103" into the ZIP code field.
Open the date picker and select any date that falls within the next 7 calendar days from today.
In the time-of-day filter, select the option labeled "Evening (after 5:00 pm)".
Ensure the therapist list is sorted by "Next available" and click the "View availability" button for the first therapist in the list.
In the availability grid that appears, click on the earliest available time slot at or after 5:00 pm on the earliest available date shown.

On the booking form, type "Jordan" in the first name field, "Lee" in the last name field, "555-123-7890" in the phone field, "jordan.lee@example.com" in the email field, and then click the button labeled "Confirm Appointment" or "Book Now".

tasks.name: Add two sliding-scale trauma therapists under $80 to the comparison list
tasks.description: Find therapists who specialize in trauma, offer sliding-scale pricing, and charge no more than $80 per session, then add the two cheapest matching therapists to the comparison list.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Find a Therapist" or "Our Therapists" link in the main navigation.
In the specialties or issues filter section, select the checkbox labeled "Trauma" or "Trauma/PTSD".
In the pricing filter section, enable the toggle or checkbox labeled "Sliding scale".
In the session fee filter, set the maximum price field to "80" dollars and apply the filter.
Use the sort dropdown above the therapist results and select the option "Price (Low to High)".
Scroll through the filtered results and click the "Add to Compare" button for the first therapist in the list.
Click the "Add to Compare" button for the second therapist in the list so that exactly two therapists are selected for comparison.
Open the comparison view by clicking the "Compare (2)" button or comparison bar that appears at the bottom or top of the screen.

tasks.name: Submit an insurance coverage question using the cheapest plan with a copay of $30 or less
tasks.description: Identify the accepted insurance plan with the lowest copay that is $30 or less, then submit a contact form asking specifically about coverage under that plan, mentioning the plan name and copay amount in your message.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Fees & Insurance" or "Pricing" link in the main navigation.
On the insurance information section, locate the table or list of accepted insurance plans and click any control (such as a column header) to sort the plans by copay from low to high.
Scan the sorted list and identify the first plan whose listed copay is less than or equal to $30; memorize or note the plan name and exact copay amount (for example, "BlueCare Basic - $25 copay").
Click the "Contact Us" or "Get in Touch" link, either in the main navigation or the page footer.
On the contact form, select "Insurance or Billing" from the reason-for-contact dropdown menu.
Enter "Alex" in the name field, "alex.taylor@example.com" in the email field, and "555-987-6543" in the phone field.

In the message text area, type a question that includes the name of the selected insurance plan and its copay amount, for example: "I have [PLAN NAME] with a [COPAY AMOUNT] copay. Can you confirm if weekly individual counseling sessions are covered under this plan?".
Click the "Submit", "Send Message", or equivalent button at the bottom of the contact form.

tasks.name: Register for a social anxiety group starting next month in the 6–8 pm time slot
tasks.description: Locate a social anxiety group therapy program that begins next calendar month, select an evening session that runs between 6:00 pm and 8:00 pm, and complete the registration form for one participant using the pay-later option.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Group Programs" or "Group Therapy" option in the main navigation.
On the group programs page, use the category or topic filter to select "Social Anxiety" or the closest matching group topic.
In the start date filter, choose the next calendar month (for example, if it is May now, choose June) and apply the filter so that only groups starting next month are shown.
From the filtered results, click on the group that has a listed schedule including a session between 6:00 pm and 8:00 pm on a weekday evening.
On the group details page, in the schedule or time slot section, select the specific 6:00 pm–8:00 pm session time from any dropdown or radio button list.
Click the "Register" or "Reserve a Spot" button associated with that time slot.
On the registration form, enter "Taylor" in the first name field, "Morgan" in the last name field, "taylor.morgan@example.com" in the email field, and set the number of participants or seats to "1".

In the payment options section, select the option labeled "Pay later", "Pay at first session", or the closest equivalent deferred payment option, and then click the "Complete Registration" or "Submit" button.

tasks.name: Start booking a telehealth therapist with 4.5+ rating and weekend availability for a free 15-minute consult
tasks.description: Find a telehealth therapist with a rating of at least 4.5 stars who offers weekend appointments, and begin booking a free 15-minute consultation by selecting the earliest available weekend slot from their schedule.
steps: Navigate to the homepage and click the "Find a Therapist" or equivalent directory link.
In the format filter section, select the "Online" or "Telehealth" option.
In the rating filter, adjust the control so that only therapists with ratings of 4.5 stars or higher are shown.
In the availability filter, select "Weekends" or check both "Saturday" and "Sunday" options.
Scroll through the filtered results and click on the profile card of the first therapist who displays at least a 4.5-star rating.
On the therapist’s profile page, locate the appointment type options and select "Free 15-minute consultation" or the shortest free consult option listed.
Open the scheduling tool for that appointment type and switch the calendar view to the nearest upcoming weekend.
Select the earliest available time slot on either Saturday or Sunday, then click the "Continue", "Next", or equivalent button to move to the consultation request form.

tasks.name: Subscribe to monthly parenting-focused mental health tips
tasks.description: Sign up for the practice’s email newsletter with a focus on parenting and family mental health topics, selecting a monthly delivery frequency and providing a sample email address.
steps: From any page on the site, scroll to the footer area and click the link labeled "Newsletter", "Stay Informed", or similar if present, or locate the newsletter signup section in the footer.
In the email input field of the newsletter form, type "parent.care@example.com".
If there is a topics or interests section, select the checkbox or tag labeled "Parenting & Families" or the closest available parenting-related category.
If there is a frequency dropdown menu, open it and select the "Monthly" option.
If there is a name field, type "Jamie" in the first name field and "Rivera" in the last name field.
Click the "Subscribe", "Sign Up", or equivalent button to submit the newsletter subscription form.

tasks.name: Complete a depression self-assessment and save two recommended articles to a reading list
tasks.description: Fill out the depression self-assessment with specified responses, submit it, navigate to the recommended resources, and add exactly two recommended articles to your on-site reading list or saved resources.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Self-Assessments", "Screening Tools", or similar link in the main navigation.
On the assessments page, click the option labeled "Depression" or "Depression (PHQ-9)" to open that questionnaire.

Answer each question in the depression assessment using the middle option on the scale (for example, if the choices are "Not at all", "Several days", "More than half the days", "Nearly every day", select the second option for each item).
After answering all questions, click the "Submit", "Get Results", or equivalent button at the bottom of the assessment.

On the results page, locate the section labeled "Recommended Resources" or "Suggested Articles" and click the button or link labeled "View all" or similar to see the full list of recommended articles.
From the list of recommended articles, click the "Save", "Add to Reading List", or bookmark icon for the first article in the list.
Click the "Save", "Add to Reading List", or bookmark icon for the second article in the list so that exactly two articles have been saved.
Open your reading list or saved resources page by clicking the "Reading List", "Saved", or similar link usually found in the header or sidebar.

tasks.name: Create a safety plan with two local crisis hotline numbers
tasks.description: Locate the crisis support information, identify two different local hotline numbers, and use them to fill out and save a safety plan form on the site.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Crisis Support", "Get Help Now", or similarly labeled link, either in the main navigation or prominently on the page.

On the crisis support page, scroll until you find the section listing local crisis hotlines, and note two different local hotline phone numbers (for example, a county crisis line and a youth crisis line).
Locate and click the "Create Safety Plan", "My Safety Plan", or equivalent button or link on the crisis support page.
On the safety plan form, in the field labeled "Emergency Contacts" or "Crisis Numbers", type the first hotline number you noted along with a short label such as "County Crisis Line".
In the same section or the next available contact field, type the second hotline number you noted along with a short label such as "Youth Crisis Line".
In any field labeled "Warning Signs" or "What I Notice", type a brief example such as "I isolate and stop answering messages".
In any field labeled "Coping Strategies" or "Things I Can Do", type a brief strategy such as "Go for a 10-minute walk and call a trusted friend."
Click the "Save Safety Plan", "Save", or equivalent button to store the safety plan on the site.

entities.name: Therapist
entities.description: Individual therapist profiles used in directory, booking, comparison, and telehealth consult flows.

fields.name: credentials
fields.description: Degrees, licenses (e.g., LMFT, PhD)

fields.name: specialties
fields.description: List of specialty labels like 'Anxiety', 'Trauma/PTSD', 'Depression' etc.

fields.name: office_zip
fields.description: Used for ZIP-based search such as 94103

fields.name: standard_session_fee
fields.description: Typical 50-minute individual session fee

fields.name: insurance_plan_ids
fields.description: Array of InsurancePlan.id values accepted by this therapist

fields.name: rating
fields.description: Average star rating, e.g., 4.7

entities.name: AppointmentType
entities.description: Types of appointments available for booking (e.g., individual counseling, free 15-minute consult).

fields.name: allowed_formats
fields.description: Values from session_format enum: in_person, online

entities.name: AppointmentSlot
entities.description: Bookable time slots for therapists, used for next-7-days, evening, and weekend availability filters.

fields.name: therapist_id
fields.description: Therapist.id owner of this slot

fields.name: appointment_type_id
fields.description: AppointmentType.id for this slot

fields.name: location_zip
fields.description: ZIP for in-person sessions, e.g., 94103

fields.name: is_weekend
fields.description: True if falls on Saturday or Sunday

fields.name: is_evening
fields.description: True if start time is at or after 17:00

entities.name: Appointment
entities.description: Confirmed or in-progress bookings for individual counseling or telehealth consults.

entities.name: TherapistComparisonList
entities.description: Temporary list of therapists selected for side-by-side comparison (up to two).

fields.name: therapist_ids
fields.description: Array of up to two Therapist.id values

entities.name: TherapistSearchFilter
entities.description: Current filter and sort state for the therapist directory page.

fields.name: specialty_filters
fields.description: Selected specialty labels such as 'Trauma/PTSD', 'Anxiety'

fields.name: format_filters
fields.description: Values from session_format enum: in_person, online

fields.name: availability_days
fields.description: List of day_of_week enum values like 'saturday', 'sunday'

entities.name: InsurancePlan
entities.description: Accepted insurance plans with copay amounts shown on the Pricing & Insurance page.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Full plan label like 'BlueCare Basic'

fields.name: provider_name
fields.description: Insurer name, e.g., 'BlueCare'

entities.name: ContactMessage
entities.description: Messages submitted via the Contact form, including insurance coverage questions.

fields.name: insurance_plan_id
fields.description: InsurancePlan.id if referenced in the message

entities.name: GroupProgram
entities.description: Group therapy programs listed and filtered on the Group Programs and Group Details pages.

fields.name: start_date
fields.description: Calendar start date used for 'next month' filter

fields.name: facilitator_names
fields.description: Names of group facilitators

entities.name: GroupSession
entities.description: Specific recurring time slots for a group program (e.g., Mondays 6–8 pm).

fields.name: start_time
fields.description: Local start time, e.g., '18:00'

fields.name: end_time
fields.description: Local end time, e.g., '20:00'

fields.name: start_date
fields.description: First session date for this time slot, if distinct from GroupProgram.start_date

entities.name: GroupRegistration
entities.description: Registration records for group programs and selected time slots.

entities.name: Assessment
entities.description: Configured self-assessments such as Depression (PHQ-9).

entities.name: AssessmentQuestion
entities.description: Individual questions belonging to a self-assessment (e.g., PHQ-9 items).

entities.name: AssessmentOption
entities.description: Answer options for an assessment question (e.g., Not at all, Several days).

fields.name: value
fields.description: Numeric value used for scoring

entities.name: AssessmentSession
entities.description: A single user’s run-through of an assessment, from start to submission.

entities.name: AssessmentResponse
entities.description: Responses to individual assessment questions within a session.

entities.name: AssessmentResult
entities.description: Computed results and recommended resources for a completed assessment session.

fields.name: recommended_article_ids
fields.description: Array of Article.id values recommended based on result

entities.name: Article
entities.description: Educational resources and articles that can be recommended and saved to a reading list.

fields.name: topics
fields.description: List of topic tags such as 'depression', 'anxiety', 'parenting_families'

fields.name: is_recommended
fields.description: True if often recommended from assessments

fields.name: recommended_for_assessment_ids
fields.description: Array of Assessment.id values this article is recommended for

entities.name: ReadingList
entities.description: On-site reading list of saved articles/resources for this single user.

fields.name: article_ids
fields.description: Array of saved Article.id values

entities.name: NewsletterSubscription
entities.description: Email newsletter subscriptions, including parenting & families topic and delivery frequency.

fields.name: topics
fields.description: List of newsletter_topic enum values such as 'parenting_families', 'general_mental_health'

entities.name: CrisisHotline
entities.description: Crisis hotline entries displayed on the Crisis Support page (local and national).

fields.name: region
fields.description: City/county/region served

fields.name: availability_hours
fields.description: E.g., '24/7' or specific hours

fields.name: is_local
fields.description: True for local hotlines used in safety plans

entities.name: SafetyPlan
entities.description: Saved personal safety plan including crisis contacts, warning signs, and coping strategies.

fields.name: crisis_contacts
fields.description: List of contacts with label and phone, e.g., [{label: 'County Crisis Line', phone: '555-...'}]

fields.name: warning_signs
fields.description: List of early warning sign descriptions, e.g., 'I isolate and stop answering messages'

fields.name: coping_strategies
fields.description: List of coping actions, e.g., 'Go for a 10-minute walk and call a trusted friend.'
